description_long: This is a carved stone block with a roughly L-shaped profile, featuring a slanted, upright back and a flat horizontal base. The front face is adorned with a relief of a stylized tree or plant motif, while one side displays a carved human figure in a robe and headdress. The stone surface is weathered, with visible chips, cracks, and signs of age, especially along the edges and corners. The back and one side are plain and unadorned, showing the natural texture of the stone. The overall color is a light beige or off-white, typical of limestone or similar sedimentary rock. This object appears to be a decorative or commemorative element, possibly from a historical or archaeological context.
description: A weathered, L-shaped stone block with a carved plant motif on the front and a robed human figure on the side, showing signs of age and chipping.
description_view_0: The front view shows the carved plant motif in relief on the upright face and the flat base, with visible surface wear and chipping.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the carved human figure in a robe and headdress, as well as the thickness and depth of the block.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a plain, unadorned stone surface with natural texture and no carvings.
description_view_3: This side view again shows the carved human figure, with the upright back and flat base visible, emphasizing the block's L-shape.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or archaeological site
attribute_installation_method: set directly on compacted earth or stone foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Chipped corners and edges, with surface wear and minor cracks indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible carvings include a stylized plant motif on the front and a robed human figure on the side.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Visually appears isolated, likely intended as a standalone commemorative or decorative element.
